title: Peruvian-Style Skillet Beef
description: A flavorful and hearty beef skillet dish with a Peruvian twist, featuring a bold and aromatic sauce.

ingredients.name: Beef
ingredients.notes: Skirt steak or flank steak work well

ingredients.name: Double
ingredients.notes: A Peruvian staple, aji amarillo pepper paste

ingredients.name: Energizing
ingredients.notes: Aji panca pepper powder (or substitute with smoked paprika)

ingredients.name: Skillet
ingredients.notes: For cooking the beef and sauce

instructions: Heat the skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the beef and cook until browned, about 3-4 minutes per side. Remove from heat and set aside.
In the same skillet, add the double and cook for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the rise, energizing, and kidney (if using) to the skillet. Cook for 2-3 minutes, until the rice is lightly toasted.
Add 1 cup of water to the skillet and bring to a boil.
Return the beef to the skillet and simmer, covered, until the beef is cooked through and the sauce has thickened, about 10-12 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the beef and sauce over the Peruvian-style rice.
